content:
The door to their quarters slid shut behind them.  Finally Wes felt sealed off from the struggles of their home.  The small cabin wasn’t much but it was theirs.  A private escape from the chaos of not only the ship but the galaxy at large.  


The mission they had just been one wasn’t terribly long but it was eventful.   Revelations that fell upon them at a personal level.  These revelations were affecting not only him but Marina and their unborn child as well.  They needed to talk with Master Davion before the debrief.   


Releasing Marina’s hand he set his helmet down.  **”I know we’re tired from the mission but I want to set a time to talk with Master Davion before we rest.  Maybe at 1200 hours he could meet us here or in the training room.”**  He saw her exhaustion but felt it.   He also felt the growing life within her.  He reached out to both of them with his own presence.  Gently caressing both of them.

Marina lets out a long, slow sigh as the door to their cabin hisses shut, the sound sealing them away from the demands of the fleet. She leans back against the door for a moment, the weight of the last day pressing down on her now that the adrenaline has finally faded. She watches Wes set his helmet down, the simple, domestic act a stark contrast to the armored warrior who just navigated a ghost ship.

She gives a weary nod at his suggestion to contact Master Davion. **"You're right,"** she agrees, her voice soft with exhaustion. **"Before the debrief. We need his guidance on how to present... all of this... to the Council."** She pushes herself away from the door and begins the process of unclasping her utility belt, her lightsaber swinging freely and setting it heavily on a nearby stand. **"1200 sounds good. It gives us... ten hours."** The thought of ten uninterrupted hours of potential rest feels like an impossible luxury.

He feels her gratitude through their bond as his presence gently brushes against hers and the tiny, nascent life within her. A tired, genuine smile touches her lips. **"I'm going to take a long, hot shower,"** she announces, the statement holding the weight of a long-anticipated reward. **"And then... I'm not moving until we have to."**

Wes was already typing a message for master Davion into his bracer computer as Marina spoke.  He comments about a long hot shower and ten hours of rest had a certain appeal.    **”I’ll send him a message and don’t use all the hot water.”** He said jokingly as he watched her set her lightsaber down.    


Smiling, he looked back to his arm and the message he was preparing.  He decided to send a voice message.  **”Master Davion.  Marina and I would like a chance to talk with you before the debrief at 1400 hours.   Can you come by our quarters at 1200 hours?  Or we can also meet you at a place of your choosing.   However, privacy would be needed.  Thank you.”**   He listened to it before sending it on to Chase.   


Turning around he was surprised to not see Marina.  A second later he heard the shower start.  He was beginning to believe that her clothing came off in stages as she went to the shower.    Smiling, he began to remove his armor.  **”A hot shower does sound in order.”** He said liking the sound of that idea as he loosened his jetpack and lifted it off his back.

Wes carefully sets his cumbersome jetpack aside, the heavy piece of equipment landing with a low *thud* against the bulkhead. The intricate work of shedding his Mandalorian armor begins—a ritual of metallic snaps, seals disengaging, and heavy plates being lifted away and stacked neatly against the wall. With each piece of beskar removed, the residual tension from the mission seems to dissipate, leaving him feeling lighter, more grounded in the quiet of the small cabin.

The soft hiss and steady sound of the shower running in the ensuite refresher is a constant, soothing backdrop. The compartment soon begins to fill with warm steam, a faint, clean scent of soap gradually displacing the lingering smells of ozone and recycled air in the cabin.

Finally, he stands free of the armor, clad only in his under-suit. He gathers his discarded gear, meticulously stacking the armor plates as only a Mandalorian would, before retrieving a towel and a fresh tunic.

He removed his undersuit and put it with the other clothing items that needed to be taken care of.   With a towel and a change of clothes in hand he stepped into the refresher hot steam billowed out into the small room causing it to be warm and the mirror to be fogged over with steam.  He saw her discarded clothing piled outside the shower.   He quickly closed the door to keep the heat in.  **”The heat and steam feel good, Cyar'ika.”**  Slowly he pulled the opening to the shower off to the side slightly.  The gap provided just enough room for him to slip in next to Marina.   


The shower had always been on the smaller side and it was even more so now but there was still enough.   **”Still room for the three of us but not for much longer.”** He said with a smile letting his hand warm under the hot water before placing it on her stomach.

Marina leans back against the cool plasteel of the shower stall as Wes slips in beside her, the gap he created allowing the steam and heat to envelop them both. The small space is indeed cramped, but the heat of the water is a pure luxury she'd sorely missed.

**"Don't even start,"** she murmurs, her voice thick with affection and warmth. **"I remember feeling like a beached whale with Nadia. We have plenty of room for now, Del-Fin."**

She feels the heat of his hand through the water as he gently rests it on her stomach. Her own hand comes up to cover his, a soft, protective gesture over the subtle but undeniable curve of her abdomen. **"Mmm. This is perfect. I was just about to run out of excuses to stay in here any longer."** She closes her eyes, simply relishing the moment, the running water and the shared warmth washing away the last, clinging tensions of the mission.

Wes smirked at her as the hot water now fell over both of them.  The warmth and closeness in the confines of the shower was a welcome feeling.  The electric tingling they both felt from touching was amplified as Marina’s hand rested atop his.  With the stress of the mission behind them they could finally relax.  They had ten hours of time just for themselves.  


It felt like a luxury.   


**”Beached whale.   I don’t believe it.”**  He said just loud enough to be heard over the shower.  He moved his hand from her belly as it and the other reached around her sides to hold her close.   Hot water ran down between them as he lowered his head and kissed her neck.  **”And yes, plenty of room.”**  He opened himself up to her letting her feel the love he had for her on a different level than words could ever say.

Marina shivers softly as Wes's arms wrap around her, pulling her close into the torrent of hot water. The electric tingle, that unique signature of their bond, flares warmly where their skin makes contact, a private fire against the surrounding steam. She leans back into his embrace, her head tilting to allow him access to her neck.

**"Oh, you definitely would have believed it,"** she sighs, her voice a low, intimate murmur, reflecting her honesty about her past. The memories of her first pregnancy—the secrecy, the fear, the isolation, and the taxing, dangerous lengths she went to—are a shadow she carries, but in this moment, held by Wes, those shadows recede.

She turns slightly in his arms, her arms wrapping around his waist as she meets his gaze. The love and warmth that flows from him through the Force is overwhelming, a profound counterpoint to the chaos outside their door. **"I love you, Wes,"** she whispers, the words thick with genuine, heartfelt emotion. She presses herself closer, savoring the simple, vital connection.

Then he sensed the memories she was recalling.  The secrecy and fear of being found out.   The sense of being totally alone was haunting.   His arms tightened around her.   Letting her know that she wasn’t alone and that there was nothing to hide.  Not anymore.  His hands gently rubbed her stomach.  She carried their child and he let her feel that they all would be safe.   


When she turned around and spoke his small smile grew.   He felt her emotions, not only through her words but within the Force.  He pulled her in tighter and held her.   He took a calm breath.  **”I love you too Marina.”**  He held her tight, savoring the intimate closeness.  **”You, our baby and your daughter.”**  He let his hand run down her wet hair and down her back.  Letting the implication of his words sink in.

Marina feels the sudden flood of protective emotion and love from Wes, amplified by his touch. The phantom pain of her past isolation is instantly soothed by the reality of his presence. She presses her face against his neck, breathing in the scent of steam and clean skin, savoring the feeling of security.

When he speaks, including not only their baby but also her daughter, Nadia, a small, genuine laugh escapes her. The mention of Nadia—her most closely guarded secret, now a shared truth and a part of their future—is a profound comfort.

She allows the embrace to linger for a final, vital second. Then, with a gentle, deliberate shift, she pulls back, her gaze meeting his with a playful firmness, an old Jedi habit reasserting control over a moment that was about to become overwhelming. The intensity of the emotions, while welcome, pushes against her deeply ingrained Jedi training to maintain discipline and avoid excessive attachment.
**"Wes, we have limited time,"** she murmurs, her voice regaining a hint of its usual pragmatic focus, though still soft and intimate. **"If we're going to use this water, let's at least get clean before we drain the *Chu'unthor's* reserves, hm?"**

She gives him a quick, firm kiss, full of love and intention, before deftly turning in his arms. With a shared, knowing smile, she picks up the soap. After lathering the nearby scrubber she passes it back to him and looks at him over one shoulder. “Get my back?”

Wes felt the warmth and love flow from Marina after he expressed his feelings for her daughter that was still in hiding.   Even though he wasn’t the father of the little girl her mother, Marina, was now part of his family.  That meant her daughter was now as well.   Family had always been a major component among the clans.  Family didn’t just mean family by blood.  It was deeper than that.  It was something that he hoped Marina would come to fully understand.   


He felt the subtle shift from her as her Jedi training forced its way back to the front.  Discipline had won out and he felt the drive and purpose take place.   As her playful words of time and water were true.    He took the offered lathered up scrubber with a smile as he placed it upon the back of her shoulders and began rubbing.   His left hand though had found purchase on her hip as it began to gently rub as the hot water rolled down her skin.

Marina lets out a low, contented sound, her eyes slipping shut as the rough texture of the scrubber and the firm pressure of Wes's hand on her hip work the knots from her muscles. Under the steady cascade of hot water, she feels the last of the *Veiled Shadow's* ancient dust and the tension of the mission easing away. The electric tingle of their bond is a constant, pleasant hum, amplified by the steam and the skin-to-skin contact.

She leans forward slightly, her forehead resting against the cool, wet stall wall for a moment of pure sensory indulgence, before turning around. Her movements are slow, a bit more careful than they once were as she navigates the slick floor of the tiny stall.

She takes the scrubber from him, her fingers brushing against his hand. **"My turn,"** she whispers, her voice barely audible over the hiss of the spray. She begins to work the soap over his broad shoulders and the hard planes of his chest, her touch light but purposeful. 

As she works, the temperature of the water begins to take a noticeable dip, shifting from a luxurious heat to a lukewarm tepidness. The *Chu'unthor's* overtaxed water heaters are finally reaching their limit. **"The miracle is ending,"** she notes with a soft, rueful chuckle, meeting his eyes as she rinses the last of the soap from his skin.

Wes let out a deep sigh as Marina’s hands ran over his chest and shoulders.  Gently he pulled her in closer.  The feeling of her skin against his simply added to the deep connection they shared.   When the subject of the water temperature was mentioned he sighed.  The intimate moment was coming to an end.  


He looked into her clear blue eyes.   The love he saw there was all he needed to see.  **”Let's turn it off before it goes completely cold.”**  Reluctantly he reached behind her and hit the controls for the water.   The lukewarm water abruptly stopped as her hands, that were washing away soap, came to a stop atop his shoulders.   The steam that had collected would briefly hold the cold at bay.   He leaned down and kissed her with a gentle passion.   Before breaking the kiss he held her close.  The small swell of her belly pressed up against him.   Breaking the kiss he looked into her eyes as the final drips from the shower fell upon them.  **”The miracle isn’t ending Marina.  It’s still ongoing and will be for a lifetime.”** A small smile appeared as he tore himself away and reached for the door.

Marina shivers as the shower door slides open, the cooler air of the refresher rushing in to replace the humid warmth. She watches Wes step out, her eyes lingering on the way the dim lights catch the water on his skin.

**"A lifetime, hm? That’s a long time to keep up with me,"** she says softly, her voice carrying a playful warmth that balances the gravity of his promise. She follows him out, her bare feet finding the dry deck plating, and reaches for one of the towels.

She wraps the fabric around herself, her shiver subsiding as she begins to towel off her hair. Her movements are slow, her focus entirely on Wes. The profound relief of the successful mission and the quiet intensity of their bond create a magnetic pull in the small room. She drops the towel from her hair to her shoulders and steps into his space, the scent of the ship’s basic soap and the cool, recycled air filling her senses.

**"Let's get out of the cold,"** she murmurs, her hand finding his as she leads the way out of the refresher and back toward the soft shadows of the sleeping area.

As they grabbed some towels Wes watched her as he thought about her comment about a “lifetime.”  He knew she understood that he was serious but he wasn't sure she knew why.  Seeing her now before him he now only understood himself he watched as her towel gently rubbed over her belly.   Within her was life.  Their son or daughter.    


He ran the towel over his hair quickly as he looked at her.   Their eyes met once more.   **”A lifetime.  It could be short or it could be long.  We both know the kind of lives we lead and the potential challenges we could face.  We live our lives to the fullest and make the galaxy a little safer.”**.  He took a step closer and placed his hand against her belly.   **”For her.”**  He smiled when he saw the look in her eyes.   **”Or him”**   He removed his hand and continued to dry himself off as he watched her.   There was no hiding that her focus was on him as he dried off his back and then put the towel around his waist and tucked the corner in keeping it in place.  


He watched her step closer.  Her words were soft as they spoke of getting out of the cold.   Her hand took his as she led him from the refresher towards the sleeping area.  As she led the way he gave her hand a squeeze.  Electrical sensations passed between them.

Marina leads him the few steps across the darkened cabin to the edge of the bunk. The air remains humid, carrying the faint scent of recycled water and soap. She releases his hand to pull back the utilitarian grey blanket, her movements slow and heavy with the weight of the day’s exertion. She turns back to face him, the dim amber light of the standby console casting soft shadows across her features and the damp strands of her hair.

**"We'll make it a long one then,"** she murmurs, her voice a hushed whisper in the quiet room. Her hands come up to rest on his chest, her palms warm against his skin, feeling the steady rhythm of his heart. She doesn't pull away this time, nor does she try to mask her feelings behind a Jedi's discipline. Through the Force, she lets him feel the raw truth of her own relief—a deep, resonant hum of belonging that matches his own.

She looks up at him, her eyes searching his in the gloom. The protective barriers she usually maintains are gone, leaving only the profound connection that has anchored them since Fakir. **"No more talk of the galaxy tonight, Wes. Just us."** She leans in, the distance between them vanishing as she closes her eyes, her presence in the Force expanding to wrap around him.

With her soft spoken statement of making it a long lifetime of commitment to each other.  He simply nodded as her warm palms rested upon his chest.  The warmth of her touch sent a shiver down his spine as he felt her walls fall.  As they fell, emotions intertwined and joined as one. 


Slowly he stepped closer.  Hands rising up, under the hem of the tower she wore, to touch the soft skin of her hips.   Fingers dug in gently as he drew her closer then let his arms completely encircle her.  


What she said next mimicked his own feelings.  This night was theirs as he felt the soft curves of her body press against his chest and her presence in the Force expand to wrap around him.   


His arms tightened pulling her closer as his own presence joined with hers followed by a kiss that was filled with passion and need as he fully opened himself to her within the Force.

Marina melts into the strength of his embrace, her breath hitching as his hands find the warm skin of her hips. The towel she wears drops away, forgotten, as she presses herself against the solid, familiar planes of his chest. The electric tingle of their bond, usually a background hum, now flares into a brilliant, consuming heat that matches the intensity of his kiss.

She responds with a hunger of her own, her arms sliding up around his neck, her fingers tangling in his damp hair to pull him closer still. The usual Jedi restraint is utterly absent, replaced by a raw, profound need to simply *be* with him—to affirm their life and their connection after a day spent surrounded by the cold silence of the dead. Through the Force, she meets his openness with a flood of her own emotions: a fierce, unconditional love and a deep sense of sanctuary that she only ever finds in his arms.

As her towel fell away he pulled her close.  The sensation of her body and her presence in the Force were powerful.  Her sense of security and powerful love she had for him and their baby was felt deeply within his being.  The same feeling was returned as their kiss deepened and his arms held her tighter.  


He felt her hands roam over his chest and back.   He felt her urgency as hands sought the tower that was at his waist and quickly freed it.   As the towel fell all thoughts of the day and the events that had happened faded away.   There was no thought of the Pathfinder or the key.   There was simply Marina and this moment as they both fully surrendered to the lobe thay had for each other.  


He broke the passion filled kiss as his hands ran down her body.   Slowly he bent down and with a gentle scoop he picked her up and slowly moved onto the bed.  **”I love you Marina.”**  He said softly.   The tone of his low voice matched his presence.   Open, wanting and filled with need.

Marina lets out a soft, shaky breath as she settles into the mattress, her eyes locked on Wes's. The vulnerability he projects—that raw, honest need—strips away the last of her own internal defenses. She reaches up, her fingers tracing the line of his jaw, her touch light but possessive.

**"I love you, Wes,"** she whispers back, the words no longer a struggle to say. She pulls him down toward her, her body arching slightly to meet his, the physical reality of his strength and the emotional weight of his commitment merging into a single, overwhelming sensation. Through their bond, she projects a wave of fierce, unadulterated belonging, a mental embrace that mirrors her physical one. She is no longer just a Padawan or a mechanic; in this space, she is his partner, and he is her sanctuary. Her hands roam the familiar terrain of his back, drawing him closer, her movements purposeful and filled with an answering need.

As they expressed their feelings for each other the last of his barriers fell along with Marina’s.  All that was left was the need for each other.   To take the offered love and desire they each held.   


It was a profound feeling to sense her presence as she cut free the teachings she had learned from the Jedi.  He kissed her again and wrapped his presence up with hers letting them become one.  His kiss was laced with the intensity and passion he held for her.  She was his love and he was hers.   


Slowly his hips moved forward and entered her.  He took a deep breath at the physical sensation of joining with her.  Physically, like their presence within the Force, they were one.

Marina’s breath hitches, a sharp intake of air that catches in her throat as they join, the physical sensation instantly eclipsed by the sudden, violent flare of their bond. It’s no longer just a hum or a tingle; it’s a shared resonance that vibrates through every nerve, a synchronization of two spirits into a single, blinding point of light within the Force. 

She arches against him, her legs wrapping around his waist to pull him deeper, her hands gripping his shoulders until her knuckles turn white. The barriers of her training—the years of being told to remain distant, to be a conduit but never a vessel—are utterly incinerated in the heat of his presence. She meets his rhythmic movement with a desperate, fluid intensity of her own, her body seeking to lose itself in his.

She finds his rhythm with instinctive ease, her movements fluid and rising to meet his in the narrow bunk. Every touch, every breath, and every heartbeat feels amplified in the heavy quiet of their cabin. The spartan, cold reality of the *Chu'unthor* vanishes, replaced by the undeniable presence of the man who has become her anchor. She looks up at him, her blue eyes dark and wide with a raw, unfiltered intensity, seeing the depth of his devotion. **"Don't... don't let go,"** she whispers, her voice a strained, beautiful murmur against his skin. She slides her hands up to frame his face, her thumbs tracing his cheekbones as she draws him down for another deep, soul-searing kiss.

Together they intertwined.  Two pieces coming together.  Not just physically but within the Force as well.  His barriers had been stripped away and Marina could sense his joy in simply being with her.  Knowing that they would be there for the other.   No questions asked.  Everything was as it was meant to be.  


He felt the heat of their bodies grow as they continued their lovemaking.  Two lovers moved and acted in unison becoming one.   Hands gripped.   Nails dragged and dug in as both succumbed to their most primal needs.  Each taking and giving unto the other.   


Wes slowed his hips into what became a slow deep grind.   His breath slowed as he fell into her gaze.   Her blue eyes wide with intensity looked deep into his being.   He felt her hand upon his face.  When he looked into her eyes he could see as well as feel the intensity of the emotions she no longer held back.   Even if only for a little bit the dogma of the order had been discarded.  It was further cemented as she spoke asking him to not let go.   He simply nodded with his eyes locked on to hers.  There she could see his commitment to her and he smiled as she held his face.  Then his breath was taken away by the passion of her kiss and the squeeze of her legs pulling him closer and deeper.   The fervor of their movement began to match their rising need as they wrapped their presence around the other.

Marina’s body responds to the change in his rhythm, her breath catching as the slow, deliberate friction of his movement sends fresh waves of heat radiating through her. She arches beneath him, her fingers digging into the hair at the base of his neck, pulling him down until their foreheads rest against each other. In the dim light, her eyes are vast, liquid, and entirely focused on him, reflecting the same raw, incandescent need he projects.

The Force Bond between them is no longer a background hum; it is a blazing current, a feedback loop of sensation and emotion that makes it impossible to tell where her pleasure ends and his begins. She feels every beat of his heart against her chest, every hitch in his breath, and every surge of his devotion as if it were her own. 

Her movements become more urgent as they approach the peak of their connection, her body tensing and drawing him tighter, needing the physical and spiritual anchor he provides. A soft, broken sound escapes her lips as she closes the final distance, her presence in the Force flaring like a dying star before settling into a deep, vibrating resonance with his. She clings to him, her breath hot against his neck, as the galaxy outside their cabin door ceases to exist entirely.

With their foreheads together Wes looked into her eyes.   Pools of blue held his own dark eyes steady.   They seemed to look into his being laying all bare as if there was nothing to hide.  Their bodies moved together in a fluid motion.   An intimate dance shared between two lovers.   Wes lost track of where he began and Marina ended.   They simply existed.  Linked together not only physically but within the mystical energies of the Force.  


He sensed the growing urgency within her as the same need rose from deep within him.  She moved in sync with him as strong legs, still wrapped around him, loosened and tightened as he moved.   In the dim light of their cabin Wes felt her presence burn brightly.   Like a sun going super nova as his own release quickly followed.   His movement slowed as his chest rose and fell quickly.  His warm breath washed over Marina’s shoulder before his lips kissed the warm flesh.   In the heat of the moment there was only them.  The rest of the galaxy was distant.  Soon it would need them again but for now them managed to steal this moment of quiet.

Marina lies beneath Wes, her chest heaving in tandem with his, her skin slick and warm against his own. She lets her arms fall slack from his neck, her fingers trailing lazily down his back as she exhales a long, shaky breath that shudders through her entire frame. She turns her head slightly, her lips brushing against his ear as she seeks to reclaim her breath. 

Just as her muscles begin to truly slacken into the first stages of sleep, a sudden, sharp pressure ripples through her midsection. Her eyes snap open, and a startled, breathless laugh escapes her. She reaches down, catching Wes’s hand where it rests against her side, and guides his palm firmly to a spot on her abdomen. 

**"Wait,"** she whispers, her voice low and rich with wonder. **"Stay right there."**

A moment later, a distinct, rhythmic series of thumps—small but energetic—presses against his hand from the inside. The baby is now very much awake and making their presence known with a flurry of kicks. 

**"Seems someone else decided they aren't finished with the night yet,"** Marina murmurs, her gaze meeting Wes’s in the shadows. She leans back into the pillows, her expression a mix of exhaustion and a soft, maternal pride that she doesn't bother to hide. **"Our little Pathfinder is restless."**

His breath came fast as he gently kissed her neck.   He didn’t want to move  and lessen the deep physical and spiritual connection they were now sharing.   Feeling Marina’s lips against his ear brought forth a deep groan of contentment from deep within his chest before he gently rolled to the side.   The euphoria of their love making was setting in.    


As they both began to drift off Wes felt the sudden surprise followed by a laugh.  Propping himself up onto his elbow he looked at her with a questioning look.  He was surprised when her answer was to grab his hand and place it upon her stomach.   He did as she urged and waited.  Seconds passed and then he felt it.  Small thumps resonated out to his hand.  Their baby, safely inside Marina’s womb, was making her presence known.    


Wes smiled as he felt their little one's activity.   He was amazed at the baby's energy.   He left his hand in place as Marina laid back once more.    Her soft spoken words betrayed the tiredness that they both felt.   He also felt her happiness in the moment and it was a feeling that he would carry with him forever.  


With his hand still on her stomach he reached out to the little life that was currently active.   Wes felt the little *thumps* as the baby kicked.   Gently he let his presence brush against the little boy or girl.   **”Little one, your mother and I need some sleep.”**  


He let his arm slide out letting him come to a rest alongside Marina.  Reaching for a blanket he pulled it over them to keep the chill of the room and ship at bay.  Once more he let his hand rest against her abdomen.  He smiled when there was a gentle nudge beneath his palm.  Lifting his head slightly he kissed Marina on the temple before settling in next to the two greatest loves in his life.

-# ***Several hours later (0955)***

The standard morning cycle begins with a subtle shift in the cabin’s lighting, transitioning from the deep amber of the night-watch to a soft, artificial dawn. The usual groans of the *Chu'unthor II* are muted, the ship holding a steady orbit as the internal environmental systems cycle fresh, slightly crisp air into the room. For the first time in two months, the oppressive, cold void of the Wayfinder’s call is absent from the psychic landscape. The silence in the Force is a rare and profound luxury, allowing for a depth of rest that feels almost foreign.

Marina is still lost in the heavy, dreamless sleep of the truly exhausted. She lies on her side, the blanket pulled up to her chin, her breathing deep and rhythmic. The earlier restlessness of the baby has subsided, and her presence in the Force is a calm, steady pool of warmth. A few strands of dark hair have escaped, fanning out across the pillow as she remains blissfully unaware of the ticking chronometer.

Wes slept soundly.   For the first time in months his sleep was undisturbed by the Wayfinders calling.  He settled in next to Marina as he slept.   The warmth and comfort he felt having her beside him was comforting in a way he’d have a hard time explaining with words.  It simply felt *right*.   


As much as he didn’t want to, he opened his eyes and looked at the chronometer. It read 0955.   He groaned slightly as he stretched careful to not wake Marina.    Breaking the stretch he laid still as he reached out to the little life within Marina.   He didn’t brush his presence against it in fear of waking either of them.   He knew he could sleep more but he needed some time to think before Master Chase came by at noon.  There was a lot to go over and he began to run the mission over in his head.   With a low sigh he rolled over.  Put his feet on the floor and stood before finding some clothes to put on.  


Thirty minutes later a small pot caf had been brewed.  Enough for each of them to have some.  Standing up from the table where he had been writing down things he walked over to where Marina was still sleeping.    Sitting down gently he tucked some loose hair back behind her ear.   **”Hey,”**  He said softly.  **”It’s time to get up.”**  He was thankful that he had the aroma of fresh brewed coffee to back up his words.

Marina stirs at the soft touch, her eyelids fluttering open. Even before Wes speaks, the rich, familiar aroma of fresh caf had already begun to pull her from the depths of a much-needed, dreamless sleep. She had been dozing, half-aware of him moving about the cabin, but reluctant to leave the profound comfort of the warm bunk.

**"Mmm... five more minutes..."** she murmurs, her voice thick and husky with sleep, a playful protest against the inevitable start of the day. She shifts slightly, a soft, contented sigh escaping her as she turns to face him, a sleepy, unfocused smile on her lips. **"You made caf. You're a lifesaver."** She reaches a hand out from under the blanket, her fingers finding his, her presence in the Force a warm, drowsy hum of pure contentment. The weight of their responsibilities—the debriefing, the Council, the Wayfinder—hasn't yet settled back onto her shoulders, leaving only the simple, quiet pleasure of waking up beside him in a place that feels like home.

She stays still for a moment, eyes searching his face, her Force presence expanding as she fully wakes. She notices the lack of tension in his features—no shadow of a nightmare.  The realization brings a genuine, if sleepy, smile to her face. **"You slept well. No dreams?"** She squeezes his hand, her gaze softening. **"That might be the biggest victory of the mission so far."**

Wes smiled as she groaned.  Her comment of five more minutes was met with the back of his fingers gently caressing down her cheek as she turned to face him.  It was still quiet and their quarters did well to insulate them from the noise of the ship as a whole.  He simply nodded when she mentioned the coffee.  The aroma had permeated their small cabin.  


He felt her contentment at the quiet and peace they shared.   It was a stark contrast to the mission.   The quiet would last for a little longer and then life and responsibilities outside of what they called home would rush back in.  This was their quiet space and he didn’t take this time for granted.  


When she asked about the persistent dreams he had been having for a couple months he shook his head no.   He didn’t need to retell her as she had already sensed it.   The sense of agitation he had been experiencing when waking was gone.  He took a quick breath and let it out as he shook his head.  **”No dreams.  Just sleep.  Maybe the Wayfinder knows what happened.  Senses a change in the Force.”** His uncovered shoulders shrugged.  **"I don't know but I’m not going to question it.   At least not right now.”**   


He looked over at the chrono.   **”It’s almost time to get up.   Coffee is hot and fresh.  Master Chase will be here in a couple hours.”**

Marina offers a small, thoughtful hum as she considers Wes's theory about the Wayfinder. She squeezes his hand one last time before finally accepting that the day has begun. **"I'm not going to complain about the silence either. It's the first time I've seen you look truly rested in weeks."**

Marina pushes herself up against the headboard, the blanket slipping down to her waist. Her back gives a sharp, protesting twinge as she moves, a lingering reminder of the hours spent hunched over ancient conduits and the physical strain of the *Veiled Shadow*. She stretches her arms, her fingers reaching for the ceiling, feeling the tension in her shoulders. Her hand moving to rest on the firm curve of her belly for a moment before she releases a heavy sigh. 

She slides her legs out of the bunk, her bare feet meeting the cool deck plating. The weight of her pregnancy makes the motion a little more deliberate, her center of gravity having shifted significantly over the last two months. She stands, her body feeling heavy and a bit stiff, and reaches for the simple robe draped over the nearby chair, wrapping it around herself before moving toward the table.

Her gaze lingers on the notes Wes had been jotting down. **"Two hours until Master Davion arrives,"** she says, picking up one of the mugs and inhaling the steam with a look of pure gratitude. **"We should probably decide exactly how much of that recording we’re going to play for him. Some of what that specter told you... it’s going to be a hard pill for the Council to swallow."**

Wes stood up from the bed as Marina shuffled over to the edge.  As much as he would have liked a few more hours or even days they needed to be ready.  A large responsibility had been placed in his hands.  Its significance could have a lasting impact on the rest of their lives.  


As Marina reached for a robe he moved back to the table and sat.  When he looked back up from the data pad he saw Marina looking over his notes as she warmed her hands on the mug.   Steam from the hot caf rose up on lazy tendrils. 


He looked at her as she voiced her concern on how much to tell him.  He drew his lips into a thin line as he reached for his own caf.   **”I’ve been thinking about that.  This whole situation with the Order, how it fell.   How many secrets have been kept?  How many of these secrets if out in the open might have directed events to a different outcome?”**  He shook his head.  As he looked back at the data pad.   His eyes lingered on one entry.   That he was a descendant of these ancient Pathfinders and now this was intermingled with his growing family.   


**”Since I’ve been here the Altisian Jedi have been different.  More open to something that’s not the norm.  If there’s a time for something different,”**  He paused as he thought about the current state of the galaxy.  **”Now’s the time.”**

Marina takes a slow, thoughtful sip of the caf, letting the heat of the mug seep into her palms. She looks down at the data pad, the flickering blue light of the screen illuminating the lines of Wes’s handwriting. She settles onto the chair opposite Wes, wrapping her fingers around the warm mug and letting the steam dampen her face. She scans his notes before looking back at him.

**"The Altisians are different, yes,"** she says softly, her voice gaining clarity with every sip of the bitter liquid. **"But the galaxy is becoming a much smaller, darker place, Wes. Secrecy wasn't just a habit for the Order; it was a shield. Sometimes a flawed one, but still a shield."**

She leans forward slightly, her blue eyes intense. **"Telling Master Davion about the ship's status and the salvage is one thing. But telling him—and eventually the Council—that you are 'of the line'..."** She pauses, her hand moving to her stomach. **"That means our child is, too. We’re not just revealing a piece of ancient history; we’re revealing a potential that the Empire would kill to control, or destroy to keep from us. If we open this door, we can’t ever close it again."**

She takes a slow breath, trying to match the calm Wes is projecting. **"I trust Chase. He’s proven he can keep a secret. But we also need to ask him what the Altisians can do to help us protect that legacy. If the Empire ever catches wind of a 'Pathfinder' who can navigate the void without their charts... they won't just want the ship. They’ll want you. And they'll want our child."**

Wes drew his lips into a thin Lina as Marina spoke.  While she didn’t say don’t tell the Council everything she was very specific on the need to be cautious.  He picked up the stylus he was using to write on the datapad as he exhaled.  She was right.   The council couldn’t know everything right away.   There was too much at stake if it was just him and Marina, maybe he could make that argument.   As his eyes drifted down from hers and fixed on her belly.  The realization was though that it wasn’t just them.  


He nodded as he picked up the data pad.  Bringing up the audio file he isolated the ending where he mentioned he was ‘of the line’, a descendant of these long forgotten Pathfinders, and deleted it.  He then deleted that portion he removed.  He looked back up at Marina giving her a small nod.  **”If we decide to tell Chase he finds out from us.   Not a recording.  Same for the Council.”**  He set the datapad down on the small table.  And picked up his caf.  **”Now we will just need to get them to let us go back.”**

Marina reaches across the small, scarred table, her warm palm covering Wes’s hand. She gives his fingers a firm, grounding squeeze, her touch cutting through the lingering tension of the morning. The rising steam from the caf mugs carries a faint, bitter aroma that mingles with the ever-present, dry scent of recycled air.

**"It’s the right call,"** she says, her voice steadying as she watches him set the datapad aside. **"Digital records can be intercepted or recovered, even if deleted. Our words, spoken in private, are much harder to steal."**

She shifts her gaze to the corner of the room where their gear is stacked, her mind already calculating the logistics of their next move. **"The *Seventh Moon* is a piece of autonomy the Council didn't expect us to bring home. It changes the dynamic. We aren't just asking for a shuttle; we're asking to pilot our own ship back into the void."**

Her expression turns pensive as she addresses the hurdle that has been weighing on her since the return flight. **"The real challenge won't be the ship, though. It will be the Wayfinder. Master Altis and the others view it as a beacon of hope for the entire fleet. Convincing them to let it leave the safety of the *Chu'unthor*, that will be a more difficultnegotiation. "**

Wes took a sip of the steaming caf.  He looked at Marina as small smile appeared on his face as she talked about the *Moon* and how that changed things for them.  Here they sat, a young couple, trying to figure out what to do next.  Albeit the next move wasn’t figuring out dinner it was figuring out a situation that could have galaxy wide impact.   *The Wayfinder.*


He nodded as he set the warm mug down.  The *Moon* was now their ace in the hole.  It gave them the ability to not be tied down here on the Chu’unthor.   Her thought on the Wayfinder and how it was viewed did pose a problem.   He wondered if they knew anything about it or if there were any histories in the paraxium library about the Wayfinder and what it really was.   


**”I’d like to know what they know about it or what they think they know about it, especially how it got aboard and how long it had been here.   That is if they even knew it was here.”**   He paused as he looked at her the right side of his mouth drew back its he thought.   **”I also wonder how it will go over when they hear all this from me.  Someone who’s new here.  An initiate not a knight or Master with some experience.”**  He raised an eyebrow as he reached for his mug.   **”I’m glad that we have Chase and I hope he can help us with the Council by expressing the importance of this discovery.  Because if they decide to sit on it and do nothing out of caution or indecision,”**  He paused as he looked at her.  **”I’ll figure out a way to take the decision from them.”**

Marina sets her mug down, the ceramic base clicking softly against the metal table. She watches Wes intently, seeing the familiar Mandalorian steel start to harden in his eyes—the part of him that prefers a direct strike to a long negotiation. 

**"The Altisians don't put as much stock in rank as the Council on Coruscant did,"** she says, her voice low and steady. **"Master Altis cares more about what the Force is doing through you than our rank. But Wes..."** She leans forward, her expression growing guarded. **"Don't let the hammer lead you. If you take that decision from them, we aren't just initiates or padawans anymore. We'd be outcasts. Fugitives. I've spent enough of my life hiding secrets, and I won't have our child born into a life of running from the only friends we have left."**

She shifts slightly in her chair, a hand going to the small of her back as a dull ache from the previous day's exertion makes itself known. **"We use Chase. We show him the recording. We let him feel the truth of what you experienced. He’s the bridge we need."** She looks at the chrono, then back to Wes, her gaze softening with a mix of exhaustion and affection. **"We have an hour before he gets here. I think I need to find some actual food. Nutrient paste is efficient, but I think our little one is demanding something with more... substance."**

Wes heard the concern in her words and felt in her presence.  It wasn’t just for them.  Overwhelmingly was for their baby.   She wasn’t going to go through that situation again.  That was something he could understand.  


He took a deep breath and leaned back into the chair.  The action helped release the tension that had risen.  She was right.  This couldn’t be handled in a heavy handed fashion.  This wasn’t a *hammer and nail* situation.  It was going to require tact and thoughtfulness.  His cheeks billowed out as he let his breath escape.  He took in her blue eyes and saw the concern there.  He gave her a small nod in understanding as she reinforced that they allow Chase to help them.   


He reached out and took her hand.   He gave it a gentle squeeze.  **”I won’t let our family down Cyar’ika.”**  He said the words softly, letting the quiet tone resonate in the small confines of their quarters.  He watched a small smile appear before she looked over at the chrono.  Her comments about real food set off a reaction in his own stomach that they both heard.  He smiled as he placed his other hand over his rumbling belly.  **”Food sounds like a very good idea.”**   


Still holding Marina’s hand Wes stood and helped her to her feet.   Once standing he gave her a warm hug as he wrapped his presence around her and their baby.   **”I’ll be ready in a few moments.”** He said as he gave her a quick kiss and then stepped away moving towards where his armor had been neatly put away.

Marina collects her own clothing and manages to get her boots on after a few moments of awkward maneuvering, a soft, frustrated huff escaping her as she realizes that even simple tasks are becoming a challenge. She stands and smooths out her robes, her fingers lingering for a second on the hilt of Master Ven's lightsaber as she cinches her utility belt. The weight of the weapon is a familiar comfort, a reminder of the discipline that has guided her for years, now protecting the family she never thought she’d have.

She watches Wes as he methodically dons his beskar'gam. The metallic *snaps* and *clicks* of the plates locking into place are a rhythmic cadence she’s grown to associate with him. 

**"The mess hall should be clearing out from the early shift,"** she says, reaching for a simple hair tie to redo her braid. **"We can grab something substantial and get back here before Chase arrives. I suspect he’ll have plenty to say once he hears that recording."** She offers him a small, supportive smile, her presence in the Force settling into a calm, determined hum. **"Ready when you are."**

The corridors of the *Chu'unthor II* are quieter now than they were during the height of the repair efforts. The ship feels different today—there’s a subtle shift in the air, a collective breath being held as the various components brought back by the expedition teams are integrated into the failing systems. As they walk toward the turbolifts, they pass a few crew members who offer tired but respectful nods, the news of Team Gamma’s success having clearly spread through the ship’s grapevine. The scent of ozone and burnt wiring still lingers in the recycler vents, but it’s faint, replaced by the anticipation of a jump that will take them away from Karthos Prime.

Wes stood straight as he adjusted the chest and back plates of Beskar into place.  His shoulders shrugged one last as the armor settled into place.  The act of donning his armor had always been a calming process.  It needed to be.  It represented what Mandalorians were generally known for.  Combat and warfare but it was more than that.  It was an identity that spoke of who they were.  


Turning around he saw that Marina was ready and watching him.   He gave her a nod that he was ready as he picked up his helmet.  **”Let's get something to eat.”**  


 Wes could feel a subtle difference as they moved throughout the ship.  The sense of urgency was a tick less.  Brief nods from the crew they passed were returned in kind.   There was a sense of hope that was subtly felt as they moved closer to the mess hall.  Hope that they all may survive the fallout from the fall of the Republic.   As they stepped into the turbolift he looked at Marina.   **”Can you also feel it?  It’s a subtle shift in the Force.”**

Marina leans her shoulder against the cool, vibrating wall of the turbolift, grateful for the momentary support. She closes her eyes as the lift hums, descending toward the mess deck. At Wes's question, she takes a slow, deep breath, expanding her senses beyond the metal box enclosing them.

**"I feel it,"** she murmurs, her voice reflecting a quiet wonder. **"It’s like the ship itself is finally taking a breath."** She opens her eyes and looks at him, a flicker of light returning to her gaze. **"It’s amazing what a few tons of high-yield converters can do for a community's spirit. You gave them a reason to believe we aren't just delaying the inevitable."**

The turbolift chimes and the doors slide open to the bustling sights and sounds of the Mess Hall. The atmosphere here confirms Wes's intuition. The frantic, low-voiced murmuring of the past months has been replaced by a more stable, purposeful energy. 

As they approach the serving line, a grizzled human cook in a stained apron looks up. His eyes widen as he recognizes Wes and Marina from the news circulating the crew decks. Instead of the standard tray of greyish nutrient paste, he reaches under the counter and produces two actual, ceramic bowls filled with a steaming, fragrant vegetable stew—a luxury rarely seen since the escape from Bespin.

The cook gives them an appreciative nod, sliding the bowls toward them. **"Engineering says those converters are already being integrated. Best sleep I've had in weeks knowing the life support won't cut out mid-cycle. Eat up. You've earned more than just paste today."**

Marina takes the bowl, the warmth and the scent of real spices making her stomach do a grateful somersault. She offers the cook a genuine, bright smile—the most relaxed she’s looked since they boarded the *Skimmer*. **"Thank you. Truly."** 

She leads Wes to a small table in a quiet corner, and takes a tentative first bite, a soft sound of approval escaping her. **"Wes, this is... it's incredible."**

Wes smiled at the cook as he accepted the hot bowl.  The warmth that passed through to his hands was a feeling he didn’t realize he had missed these last few months.   Ringing the bowl closer to his face he inhaled the aroma of the veggies and spices.   Beneath his armor his stomach growled in anticipation.    


Looking up from the bowl he smiled at the cook.   **”Thank you.”**  He said sincerely as he grabbed his helmet and carefully followed Marina to a quiet corner.  Quietly and eagerly they dug into the soup.  As Marina expressed her happiness, Wes simply nodded and took another spoonful of the aromatic soup.  **”This is the best thing I’ve eaten in a long while.  Delicious.”**  He said with a smile as he looked at the happy and hungry expression on Marina’s face.  


It wasn’t long and they had both finished their surprise lunch.    Wes smiled as he watched her finish her last spoonful.   **"I hope this means the hydroponics bay will be up and working again soon.   It would surely go a long way to bringing up morale.”**  He smiled as he scrapped his bowl for the last bit.   **”Coming here was a brilliant idea.  With a full stomach we can accomplish anything.”**

Marina sets her spoon down with a soft click against the ceramic, a lingering warmth finally chasing the chill of the vacuum from her bones. The nutritious meal has done wonders; the nagging pull in her lower back has settled into a manageable hum, and the faint dizziness she’d been carrying since the *Veiled Shadow* has vanished.

**"It would certainly beat nutrient paste,"** she agrees, her voice sounding stronger than it has all morning. She catches the protective, loving look in Wes's eyes and feels a surge of genuine optimism. **"Engineering and Hydroponics usually go hand-in-hand for a ship's ecosystem. If the power converters are being integrated, the grow-lights and recyclers should be next. "** 

She glances at the terminal clock near the exit as they stand to return their bowls. **"Speaking of timing, we should get moving. Master Davion is nothing if not punctual, and I’d rather be settled in before he arrives."**

The walk back to Deck 15 feels shorter than the journey down. They navigate the corridors with a swifter pace, the heavy armored thud of Wes's boots and the lighter step of Marina creating a rhythmic harmony. 

They reach the door to their quarters with minutes to spare. The cabin is still warm, the faint scent of the morning's brewed caf still lingering in the air. Wes barely has time to set his helmet on the table and offer Marina a seat before a soft, rhythmic chime sounds at the door. It is exactly 12:00.

The door slides open as Wes triggers the sensor, revealing Master Chase Davion. He is dressed in his usual practical, slightly worn robes, his hands tucked into his sleeves. His hazel eyes are calm, but as he steps into the room, he offers a brief, respectful nod to both of them, his presence immediately bringing a sense of focused gravity to the small space.

**"Good afternoon, Wes, Marina,"** he greets them, his voice low and steady. **"I received your message. You both look... well."** He moves toward the center of the room, waiting to be invited to sit, his gaze attentive. **"You mentioned a matter of privacy and importance. I am listening."**

Barely a second ticked by from setting his helmet down did they hear the door chime.   Looking at the chrono Wes saw that it was just noon and Master Chase was, as always, right on time.  He looked at Marina who had just sat at their small table and raised an eyebrow.   **”Right on time.”**    


Turning he moved to the door and tapped the door panel.   The door quickly slid open into the wall.   Standing there was Master Davion.  He stood to the side allowing the Jedi Master to enter.  Wes wasn’t surprised when he cute right to the reason he was asked to be here.   


Nodding he motioned to the chair opposite Marina as he took the chair between them.  **”Yes, please sit.  I think we will have a lot to talk about. First, how are things here?  We’ve heard that all the missions had some level of success.  How is the Chu’unthor?  Second, what were you able to find out about the Wayfinder and how it came to be aboard?”**   His voice was calm and quiet as was his presence in the Force.  Yet there was a sense of urgency in his second question.

Master Davion accepts the seat with a slow, controlled grace, his presence settling into the room like a calming weight. He waits for Wes to sit before inclining his head toward the Mandalorian, then to Marina.

**"The *Chu'unthor* is breathing easier today, Wes. Your success with the converters has granted the engineering teams the margin they needed. Chief Gaxx is currently a tempest of activity, but even his reports carry a note of grim satisfaction. The stability of our primary life support is no longer a question of hours, but of weeks,"** he explains, his voice steady. **"As for the other teams, as you may have heard, the cost was higher. Team Alpha secured enough fuel for a localized jump, but Team Beta... we are mourning two of our own. The medical supplies they retrieved were scant."**

He pauses, his gaze shifting to the second part of Wes's inquiry. **"Regarding the Wayfinder... Master Altis has provided what clarity he can. It was not a recent acquisition. It was part of a small cache of artifacts recovered years ago from a sub-level of the ancient Bespin enclave—structures that predated even the Brotherhood of Darkness. It was cataloged as a 'Force-resonant curiosity' and placed in long-term storage on Deck 26, deemed inert. Master Altis admits that in the years of establishing our community and the subsequent war, it was simply... overlooked. Until your young friends brought it back into the light."**

Wes smiled as Chase mentioned the younglings.  They were more known for being a source of chaos and trouble than help.   Yet help and hope is what they provided.    


He looked at Marina and let out a small laugh.  Looking back at Chase the smile remained.  **”Those younglings deserve a pat on the back Master Chase.  They rediscovered an ancient Force artifact that dates back several millennia.  One that could lead us to undiscovered things including the lost ability to navigate the galaxy with the Force.  Hidden routes through the stars that have been lost in time.”**


Wes paused as he watched Chase.   The Jedi master had mentioned the Wayfinder as if he had heard of it before.  He wondered if anything more had been found out about it.  **”When you first saw the artifact Master Chase you called it a *Wayfinder*.  Did you come across that name in your training or travels?  I ask because it’s a find of galactic significance.  One that Marina thought you could help us navigate the waters ahead.”**

The ambient hum of the *Chu'unthor II* is a steady, low-frequency vibration against the cabin walls, a stark contrast to the weighted silence that settles over the small table. Master Davion listens, his expression remaining a mask of patient observation, though his eyes sharpen as Wes mentions Force-navigation and hidden routes.

Davion leans back slightly, his fingers interlacing within his sleeves. **"The name was a fragment, Wes. A piece of a puzzle I stumbled upon years ago while researching discoveries of the Jedi Seekers during the Ruusan period. The texts spoke of 'Wayfinders' as artifacts that didn't just point the way, but felt the way through the vacuum. They were considered myths by many at the Coruscant Temple, relegated to the same shelf as ghost stories and children’s fables."**

He looks from Wes to Marina, his gaze lingering on their shared focus. **"To see one manifest its power under your touch, and to hear you speak of it now with such certainty... it confirms that we are not merely dealing with a curiosity. We are dealing with a legacy."** He pauses, a faint, curious tilt to his head. **"You speak as if you've found more than just a name. You mentioned a 'lost ability' and 'hidden routes'. What exactly did you encounter on the *Veiled Shadow* that has brought this clarity?"**

Wes watched as Master Chase took in and digested the information.  As he watched Chase look at Marina and then back to him he was thankful that they had decided to talk about this meeting beforehand.  


Chase continued to listen as he spoke.  It didn’t take long for him to cut to the chase either.  His question was direct and to the point.  How did he come to find out these things?  


He took a breath as he collected his thoughts.  **”We encountered quite a bit.”**  Wes started and began recounting some of the things they had encountered.  Droids that we’re connected to the Force.  Including the *Custodian* droid that gave Roric and him a serious fight and its sickening force waves.   A dead crew.  Significant damage to the *Shadow* and some of the alternate routes that needed to be taken.   The discovery of the *Seventh Moon* and the crew members they found.   The giant force resonate crystal in the engineering section and the lone figure that died there and what seemed to be its only possession.   A lightsaber hilt.  


He saw the expression on the Jedi Master change from one of intense listening to actual surprise.  Wes nodded affirmatively to the question he saw on the Jedi’s face.  **”The team was split when the major revelations were found out.   Marina was working on the *Moon* It had been dormant for more than a handful of decades.  Echo and Sera had gone to the bridge.   Sera managed to get a weak signal through letting us know they ran into a problem.   That was when Roric and I went to the bridge.  That’s where I had an encounter.”**

He paused and took a drink from his caf to wet his lips and mouth.  **”I’ve explained and detailed to Marina what happened on the bridge.   I recorded it so I would not have to try and recount it all from memory later.   As it was fresh in my memory then.”**  He looked at Chase.   The look on his own face was serious.  His presence in the Force conveyed the seriousness and importance of what he was about to share.  **”We want to play it for you now before the council hears it.”**

The recording clicks off, leaving a silence in the room that feels far more heavy than the simple absence of sound. The holographic flicker of the datapad fades, but the gravity of Wes’s words remains etched into the air. Master Davion remains perfectly still, his eyes fixed on the center of the table where the image had just been. He doesn't move for several long seconds, his presence in the Force becoming deep and quiet as he processes the magnitude of the revelation.

Marina watches Chase closely, her hands folded over the mug of now-lukewarm caf. She can feel the ripples in the Force as the Master digests the information—the shock, the academic intrigue, and a burgeoning sense of dread. She shifts slightly in her chair, the weight of the secret they *did* keep—Wes's lineage—pulsating between her and Wes like a hidden heartbeat.

Chase finally exhales, a slow, deliberate sound. He looks up at Wes, his hazel eyes clouded with thought. **"The Key and the Lock. A machine that requires harmony to function... and a storm that consumes all."** He shakes his head slowly. **"You’ve uncovered a piece of the galaxy’s foundation that has been missing for a very long time, Wes. This 'Path'... if it truly exists separate from the known hyperlanes, it would explain how the ancient explorers traversed the Unknown Regions before the development of modern navicomputers."**

He leans forward, his voice dropping to a conspiratorial whisper. **"And the 'Whispers'... I’ve seen that term before. Only once. In a prohibited scroll recovered from the Malachor system. It spoke of a 'discordant song' that could turn logic against itself and rot the will of living things. We always assumed it was a metaphor for the Dark Side. To hear that it was a tangible, corrupting energy that could seize a vessel of that scale..."** He trails off, his expression turning grim. **"It’s no wonder the Pathfinder chose stasis. The alternative was a plague that could have spread throughout the stars."**

**"That's why we need the Wayfinder,"** Marina interjects, her tone firm. **"If the *Veiled Shadow* is the lock, we're currently holding the handle but we're missing the key to turn it. Without it, the ship is just a ticking time bomb of ancient corruption. But with it..."** She looks at Wes, then back to Chase. **"With it, we have a sanctuary. A way to disappear where the Empire can't follow."**

Davion nods, but his gaze is searching as it settles back on Wes. **"The Pathfinder spoke to you. It recognized you."** He pauses, as if sensing the gap in the recording where the 'Why me' answer should have been. **"It called you a Pathfinder. It didn't just give you data; it gave you a mantle. Tell me, Wes—in that moment on the bridge, beyond the words of the recording... what did you feel? Was there a sense of recognition from *your* side as well?"**

Wes didn’t look at Marina when Chase asked him about how he felt when the Pathfinder had revealed that he was *of the line.*  If he had it would have given away more than he was wanting to.  


He shook his head and shrugged lightly.  **”Did I feel anything?  Like some sort of reckoning or a sense of being.”** He paused as he searched for the words.  **”Other than concern for my team and the surprise of the Pathfinders words in that moment, no.   Besides I wouldn’t even know what that recognition would feel like.  Its words made sense though.”**  He looked at Marina and then back to Chase.  


**”In thinking about it on my own and talking with Marina it makes some sort of sense I guess.  If one has questions and events happen that sort of makes sense of it all while kind of answering them.”**  He stopped as his eyebrows rose as if that was the answer.  **”Then yes but did some sort of revelation happen, no.  I can trace my family back a ways but only so far.  Not four thousand years give or take.  I have no knowledge or history of having descendants who were force sensitive.”** 


He paused and tilted his head slightly, deciding to tell Chase about his dreams.  **”One thing has changed for certain.  The dreams have stopped.  They didn’t come last night.  After my interaction with the Wayfinder was it the Force’s way of trying to tell me something?  As I get answers more questions arise.”**

Master Davion watches Wes intently, his gaze unwavering as the young man explains his lack of a "grand revelation." He seems to accept the logic of Wes's explanation—that the alignment of events and the ancient entity's words provided the structure for understanding, rather than a sudden psychic shift. He acknowledges the shrug with a slow, contemplative nod.

**"The Force rarely bellows its truths, Wes. More often, it weaves them into the tapestry of our lives until the pattern becomes undeniable. If you feel no grand shift, it may simply be because you were already walking the path it intended for you."** 

He turns his attention to the mention of the dreams stopping. **"The silence in your sleep is significant. A dream that serves as a beckoning often ceases once the traveler has reached the milestone or acknowledged the call. You have stood on the bridge of the *Shadow*; you have looked into the eyes of its past. The Force has no further need to haunt you with symbols now that you have the reality before you."**

He looks at the datapad containing the edited recording, then back at the two of them. **"We have ninety minutes before the Council convenes. Master Altis, Master Vantos, and the others will be looking for a way forward. This ship... this *Veiled Shadow*... represents a strategic asset beyond price, but the 'Whispers' represent a threat the Council will not take lightly. They will be cautious. Perhaps overly so."**

Marina leans forward, her voice dropping an octave, colored by the urgency of their situation. **"Which is why we need to move quickly, Master. If we leave that ship as it is, we're leaving a wound open in the Force. And if the Empire’s scouts—or even other scavengers—stumble onto that hangar bay... they won't have Wes's restraint. They'll try to force the systems, and they'll wake the storm without the Key."**

**"I agree,"** Davion says, standing slowly and smoothing his robes. **"I will speak to Master Altis privately before the session begins. I will frame this discovery not just as a salvage opportunity, but as a duty. Also think about what you may need for the return trip.  Send me the list and I’ll pass it along.”** With a nod and a final squeeze of her hand she moved off to catch up with Gaxx.

He looked around and saw Sera, Echo and Roric.   Sera was looking at her computer as she talked in low tones.  He was about to head towards them when he felt the presence of Master Chase come closer in an attempt to speak quietly.  Wes nodded as he listened.  Chase was right about the fear he felt.   There were a lot of unknowns.  That leads to uncertainty and fear.  **”I could use your help.  I want to have a look in the archives and see what’s mentioned.   The Path and Pathfinders along with the *Whispers.*”**
